title: Känguru der Mathematik 2024 — Felix (Pré-Écolier, Grades 1-2)
source_organization: Känguru der Mathematik (Austria) / Kangourou sans Frontières
disclaimer: Official Math Kangaroo past paper (kaenguru.at, via matematica.pt). For personal educational practice only.
answer_source: Official 2024_Answers.pdf answer table (Felix 2 row), re-read at high resolution and verified; supersedes an earlier mis-read.

questions.question_text: Which number is in the triangle AND also in the square AND also in the circle?
questions.solution: Look for the number that lies inside all three shapes at once. That number is 5.
solution_steps: Find the area where triangle, square and circle all overlap.
Read the number sitting there.
It is 5.

questions.solution: Compare the lengths of the stacked pencils. Pencil D reaches the furthest, so it is the longest.
solution_steps: Line the pencils up in your mind.
Compare where each one ends.
Pencil D is the longest.

questions.question_text: There are 8 different faces in the picture. Which face appears only twice?
questions.solution: Count how many times each face shows up. The face in option E is the only one that appears exactly two times.
solution_steps: Group the faces that look the same.
Count each group.
Only the face in E appears exactly twice.

questions.question_text: Bruno builds a big triangle using small triangles of equal size. How many more of these triangles does he need so that the big triangle is completely filled?
questions.solution: Count the empty small triangles still missing from the big triangle. He needs 6 more.
solution_steps: Look at the big triangle outline.
Count the small triangles still empty.
6 more are needed.

questions.question_text: Each of the numbers 1, 2, 5 and 6 in the picture is made by folding a strip of paper. Which strip is the longest?
questions.solution: Unfold each number into its straight strip. The strip that forms the 6 is the longest.
solution_steps: Imagine unfolding each digit back into a straight strip.
Compare the strip lengths.
The strip forming 6 is the longest.

questions.question_text: Tim sticks black and white squares on the inside of a window so this pattern emerges. Which pattern can be seen from outside?
questions.solution: From outside the window the pattern is flipped left-to-right (a mirror image). That mirror image is option D.
solution_steps: Seen from outside, the pattern is mirrored left/right.
Flip Tim's pattern horizontally.
The result is option D.

questions.question_text: Chen has these 5 baskets with 4 toys each. 4 baskets fall down and the toys mix up on the floor. Which basket did not drop?
questions.solution: The toys on the floor come from only 4 baskets. The toys that are missing belong to the basket that stayed up — basket B.
solution_steps: List the toys lying on the floor.
See which basket's toys are NOT on the floor.
That basket is B.

questions.question_text: In the table, each shape stands for a different number. What number does the star stand for?
questions.solution: Use the row and column totals to work out each shape. The star stands for 3.
solution_steps: Write the totals given for the rows and columns.
Solve for each shape step by step.
The star = 3.

questions.question_text: Marina draws five pictures, then repeats them in the same order over and over. Which picture is the 27th picture?
questions.solution: The five pictures repeat in a cycle. 27 = 5 × 5 + 2, so the 27th picture is the same as the 2nd one — option B.
solution_steps: The pattern repeats every 5 pictures.
27 ÷ 5 = 5 remainder 2.
So the 27th is the same as the 2nd → B.

questions.question_text: Ali chooses 2 numbers from the board and adds them together. How many different results are possible?
questions.solution: Adding two different numbers from the board, the possible totals are 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9 — that is 7 different results.
solution_steps: List every pair of different numbers.
Work out each sum.
The different totals 3–9 make 7 results.

questions.question_text: Chiara has a see-through cube. Inside there are 6 small cubes. What does Chiara see if she looks at the cube from above?
questions.solution: Looking straight down, the small cubes line up as shown in option E.
solution_steps: Imagine looking straight down on the cube.
Note where the small cubes sit.
The top view is option E.

questions.question_text: Anna, Bella, Cha and Dimitry each have three shapes. Each child has exactly one of their shapes the same as one of the other children. Which shapes does Dimitry have?
questions.solution: Match shapes so every child shares exactly one with another. That forces Dimitry to have the shapes in option E.
solution_steps: Each child shares exactly one shape with someone else.
Match the shared shapes between the other children.
Dimitry is left with option E.

questions.question_text: Zoran builds towers from three different building blocks. The picture shows the heights of three towers. How high is the fourth tower?
questions.solution: Find each block's height from the three known towers, then add the blocks in the fourth tower: 12.
solution_steps: From the three towers, find the height of each block type.
Add the blocks that make the fourth tower.
The total is 12.

questions.question_text: Every time Andrew throws an arrow he can hit the target. He starts with 10 arrows. Each time he hits, he gets 2 more arrows. In total Andrew throws 20 arrows. How often did Andrew hit the target?
questions.solution: Andrew threw 20 arrows but started with only 10, so he earned 20 − 10 = 10 extra arrows. Each hit gives 2 arrows, so 10 ÷ 2 = 5 hits.
solution_steps: Extra arrows earned = 20 − 10 = 10.
Each hit gives 2 arrows.

questions.question_text: These two pictures show a bridge at different times. The numbers give the distances between the cars or between a car and the end of the bridge. How long is the bridge?
questions.solution: Add up the matching gaps and car lengths from the two pictures. The bridge is 5 metres long.
solution_steps: Use both pictures to find each car length and gap.
Add the pieces across the whole bridge.
The total length is 5 metres.
